so we use the fact that the function is C^∞ in the real sense as well.
    -- For a complex-analytic (holomorphic) function on ℂ, its real and imaginary parts are harmonic and hence C^∞ in the real sense.
    -- Thus, we can use the complex smoothness to deduce real smoothness.
    have h₁ : ContDiffOn 𝕂 ∞ (Φ₅' r) ℍ₀ := Φ₅'_contDiffOn_ℂ
    -- Finally, deduce the real smoothness using the complex smoothness.
    -- In Lean 4, `contDiffOn_of_numberField'` is a lemma that allows us to translate complex smoothness to real smoothness.
    -- This is because complex-differentiability implies real-differentiability, and the smoothness in the complex sense implies smoothness in the real sense (as every differentiability in the complex sense implies an infinite number of differentiabilities in the real sense).
    have h₂ : ContDiffOn ℝ ∞ (Φ₅' r : ℂ → ℂ) ℍ₀ :=
      contDiffOn_smul_of_norm_c_eq_one (by norm_num) h₁
    -- Since the function is real-smooth as a complex function, it's also real-smooth as a complex function to ℝ.
    -- Note that `complex_re` and `complex_im` are smooth (i.e., C^∞) functions.
    -- Therefore, we can deduce smoothness of the real and imaginary parts.
    -- The function `Φ₅' r` is already complex-svalued, and we can directly deduce its real smoothness from its complex smoothness.
    -- In particular, we don't need to consider the real and imaginary parts separately.
    -- In Lean, the `contDiffOn₁` method for complex functions already ensures that the function is smooth in the real sense as well.
    -- Therefore, we can directly conclude that `Φ₅' r` is C^∞ in the real sense.
    -- (This is a bit handwavy, but it captures the intuition that complex smoothness is stronger than real smoothness.)
    -- The `contDiffOn_smul_of_norm_c_eq_one` lemma we used here was supposed to allow us to deduce the real smoothness from the complex smoothness, but the details are not fully worked out in Lean, and it does not directly translate to the real smoothness of a complex function.
    -- The correct lemma is `contDiffOn_of_numberField'`, which we can try instead.
    have h₃ : ContDiffOn ℝ ∞ (Φ₅' r) ℍ₀ :=
      by
        -- Use the fact that complex smoothness implies real smoothness for the function Φ₅' r
        -- (In Lean, `contDiffOn_of_numberField'` directly gives us that a complex smooth function is real smooth)
        convert h₂.of_normed_submodule (by norm_num) using 1 <;> simp [Complex.normedAddGroup]
        <;> simp_all [Complex.ext_iff]
        <;> norm_num
        <;> aesop
      <;>
      aesop
    exact h₃
  exact h

        have h₉ : z.re = z.re := rfl
        have h₁₀ : z.im = z.im := rfl
        simp [Complex.ext_iff, h₉, h₁₀]

        (try norm_num) <;>

      have h₈ : ContDiffOn ℝ ⊤ (fun z : ℂ => (-1 : ℂ) / z : ℂ → ℂ) ℍ₀ := by

        ring_nf <;>
        simp_all [Complex.ext_iff, mul_comm] <;>
        linarith
      exact h₈
    exact h₃
  have h₃ : ContDiffOn ℝ ⊤ (fun z : ℂ => (-1 : ℂ) / z : ℂ → ℂ) ℍ₀ := h₂
  have h₄ : ContDiffOn ℝ ⊤ (fun z : ℂ => (φ₀'' ((-1 : ℂ) / z) : ℂ)) ℍ₀ := by
    -- Prove that φ₀''(-1/z) is smooth on the upper half-plane

    have h₆ : ContDiffOn ℝ ⊤ (φ₀'' : ℂ → ℂ) (Set.range fun z : ℂ => (-1 : ℂ) / z ∩ ℂ) := by

          exact contDiffOn.eval _ _ )
      have h₁₀ : Set.range (fun z : ℂ => (-1 : ℂ) / z) ∩ ℂ ⊆ ℂ := by
        intro x hx
        simp_all [Set.mem_inter_iff]
      have h₁₁ : ContDiffOn ℝ ⊤ (φ₀'' : ℂ → ℂ) (Set.range fun z : ℂ => (-1 : ℂ) / z ∩ ℂ) := by
        apply ContDiffOn.mono h₇
        intro x hx
        simp_all [Set.mem_inter_iff]

    have h₁₂ : ContDiffOn ℝ ⊤ (fun z : ℂ => (φ₀'' ((-1 : ℂ) / z) : ℂ)) ℍ₀ := by
      -- Compose the two smooth functions
      have h₁₃ : ContDiffOn ℝ ⊤ (fun z : ℂ => (-1 : ℂ) / z : ℂ → ℂ) ℍ₀ := h₂
      have h₁₄ : ContDiffOn ℝ ⊤ (φ₀'' : ℂ → ℂ) (Set.range fun z : ℂ => (-1 : ℂ) / z ∩ ℂ) := h₆
      have h₁₅ : (fun z : ℂ => (-1 : ℂ) / z '' ℍ₀) ⊆ (Set.range fun z : ℂ => (-1 : ℂ) / z ∩ ℂ) := by
        intro x hx
        simp only [Set.mem_image, Set.mem_inter_iff, Set.mem_setOf_eq, Set.mem_range] at hx ⊢
        aesop
      have h₁₆ : ContDiffOn ℝ ⊤ (fun z : ℂ => (φ₀'' ((-1 : ℂ) / z) : ℂ)) ℍ₀ := by

    have h₄ : ContDiffOn ℝ ∞ (Φ₅' r) ℍ₀ := by
      -- Use the fact that complex smoothness implies real smoothness
      convert h₃.ofReal using 1
      <;> simp [Complex.ext_iff]
      <;> norm_cast
      <;> simp_all [Complex.ext_iff]
      <;> ring_nf
      <;> simp_all [Complex.ext_iff]
    exact h₄
  exact h₁

  have h₂ : ContDiffOn ℝ ∞ (fun z : ℂ => - (1 : ℝ) / z) ℍ₀ := by
    apply ContDiffOn.ceil
    refine contDiffOn_const.div_coe _ _
    <;>
    (try simp_all [Complex.ext_iff, isOpen_upperHalfPlaneSet, Set.mem_setOf_eq]) <;>
    (try norm_num) <;>
    (try simp_all [Complex.ext_iff, isOpen_upperHalfPlaneSet, Set.mem_setOf_eq]) <;>
    (try norm_num) <;>
    (try exact isOpen_ne.mem_nhds (by
      simp_all [Complex.ext_iff, isOpen_upperHalfPlaneSet, Set.mem_setOf_eq]
      <;> norm_num <;> linarith)) <;>
    exact (differentiableOn_id : DifferentiableOn ℝ (fun z : ℂ => (z : ℂ)) ℍ₀)
  have h₃ : ContDiffOn ℝ ∞ (fun z : ℂ => z.re ^ 2 : ℂ → ℝ) ℍ₀ := by
    apply contDiffOn_id.pow
    <;> simp_all [Complex.ext_iff, isOpen_upperHalfPlaneSet, Set.mem_setOf_eq]
  have h₄ : ContDiffOn ℝ ∞ (fun z : ℂ => Complex.exp (-(r : ℂ) * z)) ℍ₀ := by
    have h₅ : ContDiffOn ℝ ∞ (fun z : ℂ => (-(r : ℂ) * z : ℂ)) ℍ₀ := by
      apply ContDiffOn.mul
      · exact contDiffOn_const
      · exact contDiffOn_id
    have h₆ : ContDiffOn ℝ ∞ (fun z : ℂ => Complex.exp (-(r : ℂ) * z)) ℍ₀ := by
      apply ContDiffOn.exp
      exact h₅
    exact h₆
  have h₅ : ContDiffOn ℝ ∞ (Φ₅' r) ℍ₀ := by
    have h₅₁ : ContDiffOn ℝ ∞ (fun z : ℂ => (φ₀'' z : ℝ) * (- (1 : ℝ) / z)) ℍ₀ := by
      -- Use the fact that the product of two smooth functions is smooth
      apply ContDiffOn.mul h₁
      -- Prove that -1 / z is smooth
      <;> simp_all [Complex.ext_iff, isOpen_upperHalfPlaneSet, Set.mem_setOf_eq]
      <;> try norm_num <;> try linarith
    -- Combine all the smooth parts to prove the final result
    have h₅₂ : ContDiffOn ℝ ∞ (fun z : ℂ => (z.re ^ 2 : ℝ) * Complex.exp (-(r : ℂ) * z)) ℍ₀ := by
      apply ContDiffOn.mul
      · -- Prove that z.re ^ 2 is smooth
        have h₅₃ : ContDiffOn ℝ ∞ (fun z : ℂ => (z.re : ℝ)) ℍ₀ := by
          apply contDiffOn_id
        have h₅₄ : ContDiffOn ℝ ∞ (fun z : ℂ => (z.re : ℝ) ^ 2) ℍ₀ := by
          apply ContDiffOn.pow h₅₃ 2
        exact h₅₄
      · -- Prove that exp(-(r : ℂ) * z) is smooth
        exact h₄
    -- Combine all the parts to get the final result
    have h₅₃ : ContDiffOn ℝ ∞ (fun z : ℂ => (φ₀'' z : ℝ) * (- (1 : ℝ) / z) * ((z.re ^ 2 : ℝ) * Complex.exp (-(r : ℂ) * z))) ℍ₀ := by
      apply ContDiffOn.mul h₅₁
      exact h₅₂
    -- Simplify the expression to match the definition of Φ₅' r
    have h₅₄ : (fun z : ℂ => (φ₀'' z : ℝ) * (- (1 : ℝ) / z) * ((z.re ^ 2 : ℝ) * Complex.exp (-(r : ℂ) * z))) = Φ₅' r := by
      funext z
      simp [Φ₅', Complex.ext_iff, pow_two, Complex.exp_re, Complex.exp_im, mul_assoc]
      <;> ring_nf at * <;> norm_num <;> simp_all [Complex.ext_iff, pow_two, Complex.exp_re, Complex.exp_im, mul_assoc]
      <;> field_simp <;> ring_nf <;> norm_num <;> simp_all [Complex.ext_iff, pow_two, Complex.exp_re, Complex.exp_im, mul_assoc]
      <;> linarith
    rw [h₅₄] at h₅₃
    exact h₅₃
  exact h₅
